exam_name: CIPD Associate Diploma in People Management (Level 5)
exam_format: Assignment-based assessment delivered through CIPD-approved training providers
exam_price_notes: Typically £2,500–£5,000 all-in depending on training provider. Level 5 is intermediate-level.

testing_centers.notes: CIPD qualifications are delivered through CIPD-approved centres (colleges, universities, private providers). Assessment is assignment-based, not exam-based. There are no test centres; work is submitted to the approved centre.

id_requirements: CIPD membership registration required. Identity verification handled by the approved training centre.
exam_rules: CIPD qualifications are assessed through written assignments, not traditional examinations. Assignments are set by the approved centre and marked to CIPD standards. Plagiarism and academic misconduct policies apply. Some units may include group work, presentations, or workplace-based projects.
renewal_options: CIPD membership (and designation) requires annual renewal. CIPD members must demonstrate ongoing CPD activity each year to maintain professional membership. Designated members (Assoc CIPD, MCIPD, FCIPD) must record and reflect on CPD in the CIPD's online system.

prerequisites.description: Foundation Certificate in People Practice (L3) or equivalent HR experience recommended. No formal prerequisite.

retake_policy.notes: Candidates who receive a 'Refer' on an assignment may resubmit, subject to the approved centre's policy. Typically 1-2 resubmission opportunities per assessment.

domains.name: Organisational Performance and Culture in Practice

official_study_resources.title: CIPD Knowledge Hub and People Management Magazine
official_study_resources.notes: CIPD provides extensive free and member-access resources via the Knowledge Hub, including research reports, factsheets, and guides. Course materials are provided by the approved training centre.

aliases: cipd associate diploma in people management level 5
